summarization.a: Schematic diagram of a Ni(II) formate monodentate monomer, showing a single oxygen atom coordinating to the nickel center.
summarization.b: Schematic diagram of a Ni(II) formate bidentate planar monomer, where the formate ligand binds through two oxygen atoms in a planar coordination geometry.
summarization.c: Schematic diagram of a Ni(II) formate bidentate tetrahedral monomer, illustrating a non-planar coordination environment around the nickel center.
summarization.d: Schematic diagram of a Cu(I) formate dimer, showing two copper atoms bridged by formate ligands confirming a dimeric coordination structure.
summarization.e: Schematic diagram of a Ni(II) formate dimer, depicting two nickel centers connected through shared formate ligands.
summarization.f: Schematic diagram of a Ni(II) formate trimer, consisting of three nickel atoms linked in a cyclic oligomeric arrangement.
summarization.g: Schematic diagram of Ni(II) tetramer structure #1, showing a four-nickel oligomer coordinated by multiple formate ligands.
summarization.h: Schematic diagram of Ni(II) tetramer structure #2, presenting an alternative four-nickel coordination topology distinct from tetramer #1.
summarization.i: Schematic diagram of Ni(II) tetramer structure #3, illustrating a third distinct tetrameric coordination arrangement of nickel and formate ligands.

e.question: Why is the dimeric structure highlighted as significant for formate complexes?
e.answer: According to the figure caption, the dimer structure is found to be the most stable configuration for metal formate complexes. Understanding this stability is crucial for predicting how these precursors will behave during the volatilization and surface reaction steps of an ALD process. It suggests that the precursor likely exists in this bridged form rather than as an isolated monomer under experimental conditions.

a.question: What distinguishes the coordination of the Ni monodentate monomer from the bidentate variants?
a.answer: The monodentate monomer coordinates through a single oxygen atom of the formate ligand rather than two.

f.question: How many nickel atoms are linked together to form the cyclic oligomer shown in this panel?

b.question: How is the nickel center coordinated in the bidentate planar monomer?
b.answer: It's coordinated by two oxygen atoms from each formate ligand in a flat, square-planar-like arrangement.
